topTargets.name: Malachi Fields
topTargets.reasoning: Classic high-RSP-affinity reach: Consensus has him #22 Tier 3 (likely available at 1.10) but RSP DoT 87.2 ranks him as the WR4 in the entire class with a Nico Collins comp. ADP 0 (off market top 30) provides a hidden value; Pigskins WR room is deep (9) but Fields is a starter-grade plug-and-play talent.

topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #8 Tier 2 (CLE landing spot), RSP DoT 86.3 (WR5, Starter grade), MFL ADP 13.57. If he slips past 1.07-1.09 he's the cleanest board-blender at 1.10 — Tier 2 consensus AND top-5 RSP WR. Heavy WR room but Concepcion's slot/return profile is positionally distinct.

topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #13 Tier 2 (KC landing spot is elite), RSP DoT 84.6 with a Harold Fannin trajectory comp — this is the kind of RSP-loved RB a high-affinity GM grabs at 1.10 if Fields/Concepcion are off the board. RB room is 7 deep but only Penix/Pollard/Allen are core; KC backfield opens immediate volume.

topTargets.name: Skyler Bell
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 85.9 with a Ja'Marr Chase high-end comp — Waldman's #6 overall — but Consensus has him in 'Free Agents' tier (#33) and ADP doesn't list him. Pure 2.10/3.10 lottery ticket that perfectly fits high-RSP-affinity behavior; goes to taxi where the 50% cap hit is survivable on the $2.3M space.

topTargets.name: Chris Brazzell II
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #15 Tier 2 (CAR), RSP DoT 84.2 with a George Pickens comp, MFL ADP 26.47. Realistic 2.10 target — Tier 2 consensus aligned with RSP top-15, and the Carolina depth chart has WR1 reps available. Fits a contention-window team adding ceiling.

capPosture: Cap-strapped at $42.7M used with $2.3M space and 28 active — rookies MUST go taxi (50% base hit) until the 5 expiring contracts roll off.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: Consensus #21 Tier 3 (PHI), RSP DoT 84.4 'on cusp of Starter tier' with a classic move-TE profile. High-RSP-affinity GMs love converted-QB TE archetypes; if Pigskins skip TE early but Stowers slips to 3.10, the Philly target share is a real lottery ticket at the taxi cap rate.

summary: Pacific Pigskins are a contention-window roster with a deep WR/RB room but a hard cap squeeze ($2.3M space) — every rookie pick goes to taxi by necessity. With high RSP affinity (40% Waldman weight), the GM will fade pure consensus chalk and reach for Waldman-graded sleepers: Malachi Fields at 1.10 is the prototype move (Consensus Tier 3 / RSP DoT 87.2). 2.10 and 3.10 become RSP-board mining trips for Skyler Bell, Emmett Johnson, and Brazzell — high-ceiling taxi stashes that exploit the 50% cap hit while the 5 expiring contracts open active-roster space in 2027.
